assertions.evidence: The solution begins with the farmer taking the goat across, which is the only valid first move to prevent the goat from eating the cabbage or the wolf from eating the goat.
The sequence (Goat across, Return, Wolf across, Goat back, Cabbage across, Return, Goat across) consists of exactly 7 trips.

assertions.text: No items are eaten during the process
assertions.evidence: The sequence ensures that the wolf and goat, and the goat and cabbage, are never left alone together on either bank without the farmer.

assertions.text: All items are successfully moved to the other side
assertions.evidence: At the conclusion of the 7th crossing, the wolf, goat, cabbage, and farmer are all on the opposite bank.
